plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL positions to eliminate leveraged ETF risk and comply with conservative mode.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Trim CRWD to 8% to reduce single name concentration.
This plan aims for capital preservation and a large cash buffer.
The portfolio will be less volatile with tighter stops.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for approximately 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration. Expect lower volatility and smaller drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits and reduce gross leverage.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Buy TSLA at 10% and AVGO at 5% based on strong scanner setups.
Increase QQQI to 25% to blend income with growth.
This plan balances risk and growth with a moderate cash buffer.
It aims to reduce overall portfolio concentration.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality tech names. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits, maintaining broad exposure.
Add to SOXL up to 10% to lean into leveraged semiconductor exposure.
Buy TSLA at 15% and NVDU at 10% based on their high scanner scores.
Add to CRWD up to 15% to press a working position.
Sell SPAL to consolidate leveraged ETF exposure.
This plan uses leverage and concentrates in high-scoring setups with strict stops.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for approximately 4% per month, pursuing stretch growth. It embraces higher leverage and concentration in high-conviction setups. Be aware that this mode significantly increases both upside potential and drawdown risk, and leveraged ETFs are subject to daily decay and rebalancing.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce broad market concentration.
Sell all SOXL, SPAL, SMH, and CRWD to eliminate high-beta and leveraged exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income and covered-call ETF exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
It aims to park cash for yield, reducing growth-oriented positions.
A cash buffer will be maintained for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It significantly reduces growth and high-beta exposure in favor of income-generating ETFs. While aiming for steady distributions and low drawdown, remember that distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in selloffs.
